name: Alange Reservoir Loop
name_original: Rodea el Embalse de Alange — circular por paisajes tipicos extremeños
description: Cycle 43.2 miles around the Alange Reservoir, enjoying moderate road cycling through Extremaduran landscapes and historic Alange.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around La Zarza are characterized by varied terrain, including rolling hills and routes that often follow the contours of reservoirs. The region offers a mix of open landscapes and stretches through agricultural areas, providing diverse scenery for cyclists. Elevation gains are moderate, making it suitable for those seeking engaging rides without extreme climbs. The area's road network connects several towns and historical sites, allowing for rides that combine physical activity with cultural exploration. Best road cycling routes around La Zarza The most popular road cycling route is Mérida, Torremejía, and Alange Loop, a 43.6 miles (70.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a total elevation gain of 1,199 feet (365.8 meters). Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alange Reservoir Loop, a moderate 43.2 miles (69.6 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain, totaling 2,097 feet (639.1 meters), and circles the scenic Alange Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Alange Reservoir Loop via La Zarza, a 38.1 miles (61.2 km) trail leading through the reservoir's surroundings, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Road cycling around La Zarza is defined by reservoir views, agricultural landscapes, and connecting routes between towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on moderate difficulty. The routes in La Zarza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 113 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Zarza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around La Zarza?
A: There are 7 road cycling routes around La Zarza, offering diverse experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near La Zarza?
A: The road cycling routes around La Zarza feature varied terrain, including rolling hills and paths that often follow the contours of reservoirs. You'll experience a mix of open landscapes and stretches through agricultural areas, providing diverse scenery. Elevation gains are generally moderate, making them engaging without extreme climbs.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners around La Zarza?
A: While most routes around La Zarza are classified as moderate, they are suitable for those seeking engaging rides without extreme climbs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993807382/merida-torremejia-and-alange-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mérida, Torremejía, and Alange Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with a manageable elevation gain of 1,199 feet (365.8 meters) over 43.6 miles (70.1 km).

Q: What are the most popular road cycling routes in the La Zarza area?
A: Among the most popular routes are the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993807382/merida-torremejia-and-alange-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mérida, Torremejía, and Alange Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993709872/alange-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;. These routes are favorites for their scenic views and engaging terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around La Zarza?
A: Yes, many of the routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993709872/alange-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995549030/alange-reservoir-loop-via-la-zarza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir Loop via La Zarza&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic circular rides.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes near La Zarza?
A: The routes often connect several towns and historical sites, allowing for rides that combine physical activity with cultural exploration. You can explore attractions such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2372869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Castle&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Alange Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6972641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa del Mitreo and Los Columbarios Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/742763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyard Road on the Camino de Almendralejo to Torremejía&lt;/a&gt; also offers picturesque scenery.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the routes?
A: Absolutely. Cyclists can discover historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2372869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a glimpse into the region's past. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6972641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa del Mitreo and Los Columbarios Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; provides insight into ancient Roman history, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6417421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock Paintings of La Calderita&lt;/a&gt; showcase prehistoric art.

Q: What is the typical duration and distance for road cycling routes in this area?
A: Routes vary in length and time. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993807382/merida-torremejia-and-alange-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mérida, Torremejía, and Alange Loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 43.6 miles (70.1 km) and takes approximately 3 hours 23 minutes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6901117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Mérida – Alange Reservoir loop from Mérida&lt;/a&gt; is shorter at 28.2 miles (45.3 km) and can be completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in La Zarza?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse scenery, which includes reservoir views and agricultural landscapes, as well as the well-connected road network that links various towns and historical sites.

Q: Are there routes that offer significant elevation challenges?
A: While most routes are moderate, some offer more significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993709872/alange-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; features a total elevation gain of 2,097 feet (639.1 meters) over 43.2 miles (69.6 km), providing a good challenge for those seeking more climbing.

Q: Can I find routes that start from nearby towns like Mérida or Almendralejo?
A: Yes, there are routes that connect to La Zarza from surrounding towns. For instance, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6901117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Mérida – Alange Reservoir loop from Mérida&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir – La Zarza loop from Almendralejo&lt;/a&gt;, offering convenient starting points from these locations.
tips: Bridge that is at the end of the Alange Reservoir and where it continues from the Río Matachel. Depending on the time of year, it is full of water or, if it is summer, dry.
Going towards the town of Palomas you will find a series of agricultural fields that abound around the road. Depending on the time of year, the fields are an intense green, forming a spectacle.

Arriving in the town of Alange and with the Alange Reservoir at its feet, from a distance, you can see the center with the hill where the Culebra Castle resides. This elevated construction is visible above the rest of the territory.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed for racebikes and typically takes around 3 hours and 20 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as moderate. While it is mostly paved, it includes an elevation gain of approximately 639 meters, which may present a challenge for absolute beginners. Riders should have a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily on paved surfaces, ideal for racebikes. However, some routes in the region, even if 'mostly paved,' might include short unpaved segments. Expect varied gradients, with some ascents averaging around 5% and occasional steeper sections.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I experience along the Alange Reservoir route?
faqs.answer: You will cycle through typical Extremaduran landscapes, featuring picturesque views of the Alange Reservoir, oak and holm-oak forests, and pasturelands. The route offers glimpses of the town of Alange, its Roman spa, and the remains of its Alcazaba.

faqs.question: What are the main landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the Matachel River Bridge, scenic Fields of crops near Palomas, and various Views of Alange, offering perspectives of the town and its historical sites.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Extremadura offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer heat. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) are generally considered the best times, with milder temperatures and beautiful natural scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route are not detailed, the town of Alange, situated by the reservoir, would be a logical starting point with potential parking options for cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, which generally means dogs are not typically accommodated on the bike itself. If you are considering bringing a dog, it would be best to research local regulations regarding dogs in public areas and ensure they are safely secured if carried on a bike.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route around the Alange Reservoir. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the nearby town of Alange offers various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, making it a convenient base or stop for refreshments during your ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Cañada Real Leonesa Occidental, Camino del Cancho de los Enamorados, Calle Cancho de los Enamorados, Camino de la Culebra, and Camino del Encinar.
